description_long: This object consists of five irregularly shaped, dark gray stones arranged in a vertical stack. Each stone has a faceted, angular geometry with flat and sloped surfaces, giving them a roughly hewn appearance. The stones vary in size, with the largest at the base and the smallest at the top, creating a tapered, balanced column. The surfaces are matte and appear rough, with no visible carvings, veining, or artificial markings. The coloration is consistent throughout, suggesting a uniform material composition, likely granite or basalt. This arrangement is typical of decorative or functional stone barriers found in urban parks, gardens, or along pathways.
description: A stack of five dark gray, angular stones with rough, matte surfaces and a tapered, balanced form. The stones are irregularly shaped and arranged vertically, resembling a natural or decorative urban barrier.
description_view_0: A frontal view showing the vertical stack of five stones, clearly displaying their size gradation from bottom to top.
description_view_1: A side view revealing the individual profiles of each stone, emphasizing their irregular, angular shapes and the gaps between them.
description_view_2: A rear view similar to the front, showing the stacked arrangement and the flat surfaces of each stone.
description_view_3: An opposite side view highlighting the unique contours and sloped surfaces of each stone in the stack.
attribute_placement: stacked with other stones as a barrier or decorative feature
attribute_pattern: No visible pattern; surfaces are uniformly rough and unmarked, with natural angularity.
attribute_weathering: Surfaces appear rough and matte, suggesting moderate weathering but no significant erosion or biological growth.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural landscaping elements or contrasts with paved urban surfaces, depending on placement.
